behavior_summary: Handles the U8P_GASP::hatch event for a GASP egg: temporarily marks the egg item, obtains the egg's type/id, invokes a guardian-notification/bark routine referencing that id, clears the temporary mark, and destroys the egg item.

The temporary 'info' mark (info id 0x0210) and an immediate 'process exclude' call are used to alter processing state while U8P_GASP::hatch logic runs, ensuring the egg is processed in a special way during the notification.
Egg::getEggId supplies an identifier that is passed to Item::guardianBark (with a mode byte and a callback/address), which likely causes audio/notification or guardian behavior tied to the egg id.
notable_control_flow: On Event 7 (U8P_GASP::hatch): set info(0x0210) on the egg item, then call process exclude to adjust processing state.
Call Egg::getEggId(this) and store return in a local (eggId).
Call Item::guardianBark(mode=0x01, eggId, callback/address) and save its return to a temporary local.
Clear info(0x0210) by writing 0 and call Item::destroy(this) to remove the egg.
open_questions: What exact gameplay effect does info id 0x0210 represent? (temporary processing marker vs. state used by other systems) — inferred, not explicit.
What precise behavior does Item::guardianBark perform (plays a sound, spawns/alerts a guardian NPC, logs a notification)? This is inferred from name and arguments.
What is the nature and purpose of the pushed callback/address argument passed into Item::guardianBark (synchronous callback, script address, or context pointer)?
Does process exclude fully suspend other processing for this item or merely toggle a per-step filter? The exact engine semantics are not explicit here.

main_function.name: U8P_GASP::hatch
main_function.purpose: Respond to an egg U8P_GASP::hatch by marking the egg temporarily, retrieving its identifier, triggering guardian-related notification/behavior using that identifier, clearing the temporary mark, and destroying the egg item.
entry_conditions: Event 7 (U8P_GASP::hatch) — invoked when the GASP egg hatches

function_map.name: U8P_GASP::hatch
function_map.role: Handle the U8P_GASP::hatch event for a GASP egg, orchestrating temporary state, guardian notification, and egg destruction.

external_calls.purpose: Write a temporary info/metadata field (id 0x0210) on the egg item to alter its processing while U8P_GASP::hatch logic runs.
Adjust the item's processing/exclusion state immediately after setting the info flag; likely prevents other standard processing during U8P_GASP::hatch handling.
Return the egg's identifier/type; the id is used to parameterize the guardian notification.
Trigger a guardian-related reaction/notification using a mode byte, the egg id, and a callback/address; inferred to play a sound or notify/spawn guardian behavior tied to the egg.
Clear the previously-set temporary info flag by writing 0 to info id 0x0210 before finalizing.
Destroy/remove the egg item from the world after U8P_GASP::hatch processing completes.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Handles the U8P_GASP::hatch event for a GASP egg: temporarily marks the egg item, obtains the egg's type/id, invokes a guardian-notification/bark routine referencing that id, clears the temporary mark, and destroys the egg item.
